output: Batch 3 completed. `_trim_conversation` now enforces both the user-turn cap and the 80 KB serialized-history budget while trimming only at persisted user-text turn boundaries, so Anthropic tool_use/tool_result pairs stay intact. I checkpointed the batch result here and verified the trim behavior with a synthetic-history script plus an import check.
deviations: The repository already contained unrelated pending changes before this batch; they were left untouched, including `.megaplan/plans/add-tweet-thread-reply-20260408-0423/`, `.megaplan/plans/light-cleanup-of-six-bot-bugs-20260408-0250/`, `.megaplan/schemas/critique.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/execution.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/finalize.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/gate.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/loop_execute.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/loop_plan.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/plan.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/prep.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/review.json`, `.megaplan/schemas/revise.json`, `scripts/test_social_picks.py`, `src/features/summarising/summariser.py`, and `tmp_admin_chat_trim_check.py`.
Broader suite verification remains for T4; this batch only performed T3-scoped behavioral checks and an import check.

task_updates.executor_notes: Verified `_trim_conversation` now finds turn starts only on `{'role': 'user', 'content': <str>}` messages, applies the admin/member turn cap first, measures serialized history in UTF-8 bytes against `MAX_CONVERSATION_BYTES`, trims only from one persisted user-turn boundary to the next, and still runs after success-path writeback. A synthetic-history script confirmed byte-cap eviction preserves tool_use/tool_result pairing and keeps a single oversized turn instead of emptying history; the import check also passed.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed the while-loop guard remains `len(turn_starts) > 1`, so a single oversized turn is preserved, and every trim step still cuts only at persisted user-text turn boundaries rather than inside a tool_use/tool_result pair.
